---
title: "PADM 8410: Public Data Analysis"
subtitle: "Spring 2026 | Clemson University"
toc: false
---

## Course Information

|               |                                                    |
|---------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| **Instructor**| Dr. Will Horne                                     |
| **Email**     | [rwhorne@clemson.edu](mailto:rwhorne@clemson.edu)  |
| **Office**    | 230a Brackett Hall                                  |
| **Office Hours** | By appointment ([schedule here](https://calendly.com/app/scheduling/meeting_types/user/me)) |
| **Meeting**   | Tuesdays                                           |

## About This Course

This course introduces social science research techniques and data analysis, with a focus on methods relevant to public policy evaluation. Topics include:

- Probability theory and descriptive statistics
- Data visualization
- Regression models (OLS and MLE)
- Causal inference and design-based approaches (RCTs, difference-in-differences, regression discontinuity)

The course takes a hands-on approach using **R** and the **tidyverse**. All lectures, problem sets, and in-class activities involve writing and running code.

## Textbooks (all free!)

- Wickham, Cetinkaya-Rundel & Grolemund, [R for Data Science (2e)](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/)
- Dos-Santos Disorbo & Blitzstein, [Probability!](https://bookdown.org/probability/beta/)
- Gelman, Hill & Vehtari, [Regression and Other Stories](https://avehtari.github.io/ROS-Examples/)
- Huntington-Klein, [The Effect](https://theeffectbook.net/)
- Cunningham, [Causal Inference: The Mixtape](https://mixtape.scunning.com/)
